title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
The Math object provides mathematical constants and functions. It is not a constructor — all properties and methods are static.

Useful constants:
• Math.PI — π ≈ 3.14159
• Math.E — Euler's number ≈ 2.71828

Useful methods:
• Math.round(n) — rounds to nearest integer.
• Math.floor(n) — rounds down.
• Math.ceil(n) — rounds up.
• Math.trunc(n) — removes decimal (towards 0).
• Math.abs(n) — absolute value.
• Math.max(...args) / Math.min(...args) — largest/smallest.
• Math.sqrt(n), Math.pow(base, exp) — square root, power.
• Math.random() — random float [0, 1).
• Math.log(n), Math.log2(n), Math.log10(n) — logarithms.
